title: Find the mistake: two-step equations
description: Practice spotting the mistake in someone else's work as they attempt to solve two-step equations. Common mistakes include distributing incompletely and not performing the same operation on both sides of the equation.
topic_path: Different options for solving one equations

assessment_items.question_content:
Tyler tried to solve an equation step by step.

$\begin{align}
3(x+4)&=-18\\\\
3x+12&=-18&\green{\text{Step } 1}\\\\
3x&=-30&\blue{\text{Step } 2}\\\\
x&=-10&\purple{\text{Step } 3}
\end{align}$

**Find Tyler's mistake.**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: $\green{\text{Step }1}$ correctly distributed $3$ to $x+4$.
$\blue{\text{Step }2}$ correctly subtracted $12$ from each side of the equation.
$\purple{\text{Step }3}$ correctly divided each side of the equation by $3$ to get $x=-10$.
Tyler did not make a mistake.

choices.content: Tyler did not make a mistake.

assessment_items.question_content:
Taylor tried to solve an equation step by step.

$\qquad\begin{align}
7(y+4.5)&=32.5\\\\
\\
7y+4.5&=32.5&\green{\text{Step } 1}\\\\
\\
7y&=28&\blue{\text{Step } 2}\\\\
\\
y&=4&\purple{\text{Step } 3}\\\\
\end{align}$

**Find Taylor's mistake.**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: $\green{\text{Step }1}$ distributed $7$ to $y$.

This is a mistake!

$7$ should have been distributed to $y+4.5$, not just $\red y$.
Taylor made a mistake in $\green{\text{Step }1}$.

choices.content: Taylor did not make a mistake.

assessment_items.question_content:
Bryce tried to solve an equation step by step.

$\qquad\begin{align}
\dfrac83&=3\left(c+\dfrac53\right)\\\\
\\
\dfrac83&=3c+\dfrac53&\green{\text{Step } 1}\\\\
\\
1&=3c&\blue{\text{Step } 2}\\\\
\\
\dfrac13&=c&\purple{\text{Step } 3}\\\\
\end{align}$

**Find Bryce's mistake.**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: $\green{\text{Step }1}$ distributed $3$ to $c$.

This is a mistake!

$3$ should have been distributed to $c+\dfrac53$, not just $\red c$.
Bryce made a mistake in $\green{\text{Step }1}$.

choices.content: Bryce did not make a mistake.

assessment_items.question_content:
Frank tried to solve an equation step by step.

$\qquad\begin{align}
-5&=5(d+4)\\\\
\\
-5&=5d+20&\green{\text{Step } 1}\\\\
\\
15&=5d&\blue{\text{Step } 2}\\\\
\\
3&=d&\purple{\text{Step } 3}\\\\
\end{align}$

**Find Frank's mistake.**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: $\green{\text{Step }1}$ correctly distributed $5$ to $d+4$.
$\blue{\text{Step }2}$ added $20$ to the left side of the equation and subtracted $20$ from the right side of the equation.

This is a mistake!

$20$ should have been subtracted from both sides of the equation.
Frank made a mistake in $\blue{\text{Step }2}$.

choices.content: Frank did not make a mistake.

assessment_items.question_content:
Sharon tried to solve an equation step by step.

$\qquad\begin{align}
9&=-3(e-2)\\\\
\\
9&=-3e+6&\green{\text{Step } 1}\\\\
\\
15&=3e&\blue{\text{Step } 2}\\\\
\\
5&=e&\purple{\text{Step } 3}\\\\
\end{align}$

**Find Sharon's mistake.**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: $\green{\text{Step }1}$ correctly distributed $-3$ to $e-2$.
$\blue{\text{Step }2}$ added $6$ to the left side of the equation and subtracted $6$ from the right side of the equation.

This is a mistake!

$6$ should have been subtracted from both sides of the equation.
Sharon made a mistake in $\blue{\text{Step }2}$.

choices.content: Sharon did not make a mistake.

assessment_items.question_content:
Vanessa tried to solve an equation step by step.

$\qquad\begin{align}
-12&=4(f-6)\\\\
\\
-12&=4f-24&\green{\text{Step } 1}\\\\
\\
12&=4f&\blue{\text{Step } 2}\\\\
\\
4&=f&\purple{\text{Step } 3}\\\\
\end{align}$

**Find Vanessa's mistake.**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: $\green{\text{Step }1}$ correctly distributed $4$ to $f-6$.
$\blue{\text{Step }2}$ correctly added $24$ to each side of the equation.
$\purple{\text{Step }3}$ simplified from $12=4f$ to $4=f$.

This is a mistake!

$12=4f$ simplifies to $3=f$, not $\red{4=f}$.
Vanessa made a mistake in $\purple{\text{Step }3}$.

choices.content: Vanessa did not make a mistake.

assessment_items.question_content:
Timothy tried to solve an equation step by step.

$\qquad\begin{align}
9\left(2x+\dfrac13\right)&=39\\\\
\\
18x+3&=39&\green{\text{Step } 1}\\\\
\\
18x&=36&\blue{\text{Step } 2}\\\\
\\
x&=2&\purple{\text{Step } 3}\\\\
\end{align}$

**Find Timothy's mistake.**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: $\green{\text{Step }1}$ correctly distributed $9$ to $2x+\dfrac13$.
$\blue{\text{Step }2}$ correctly subtracted $3$ from each side of the equation.
$\purple{\text{Step }3}$ correctly divided each side of the equation by $18$ to get $x=2$.
Timothy did not make a mistake.

choices.content: Timothy did not make a mistake.

assessment_items.question_content:
Betsie tried to solve an equation step by step.

$\qquad\begin{align}
3(a+7)&=-33\\\\
\\
a+7&=-11&\green{\text{Step } 1}\\\\
\\
a&=-4&\blue{\text{Step } 2}\\\\
\end{align}$

**Find Betsie's mistake.**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: $\green{\text{Step }1}$ correctly divided each side of the equation by $3$.
$\blue{\text{Step }2}$ added $7$ to the right side of the equation and subtracted $7$ from the left side of the equation.

This is a mistake!

$7$ should have been subtracted from both sides of the equation.
Betsie made a mistake in $\blue{\text{Step }2}$.

choices.content: Betsie did not make a mistake.

assessment_items.question_content:
Chase tried to solve an equation step by step.

$\qquad\begin{align}
\dfrac13(g-3)&=3\\\\
\\
g-3&=9&\green{\text{Step } 1}\\\\
\\
g&=12&\blue{\text{Step } 2}\\\\
\end{align}$

**Find Chase's mistake.**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: $\green{\text{Step }1}$ correctly divided each side of the equation by $\dfrac13$.
$\blue{\text{Step }2}$ correctly simplified from $g-3=9$ to $g=12$.
Chase did not make a mistake.

choices.content: Chase did not make a mistake.

assessment_items.question_content:
Felix tried to solve an equation step by step.

$\qquad\begin{align}
2(5g-1)&=3\\\\
\\
5g-1&=1.5&\green{\text{Step } 1}\\\\
\\
5g&=2.5&\blue{\text{Step } 2}\\\\
\\
g&=0.5&\purple{\text{Step } 3}\\\\
\end{align}$

**Find Felix's mistake.**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: $\green{\text{Step }1}$ correctly divided each side of the equation by $2$.
$\blue{\text{Step }2}$ correctly added $1$ to each side of the equation.
$\purple{\text{Step }3}$ correctly divided each side of the equation by $5$ to get $g=0.5$.
Felix did not make a mistake.

choices.content: Felix did not make a mistake.

assessment_items.question_content:
Nigel tried to solve an equation step by step.

$\qquad\begin{align}
\dfrac12(2h-4)&=20\\\\
\\
2h-4&=10&\green{\text{Step } 1}\\\\
\\
2h&=14&\blue{\text{Step } 2}\\\\
\\
h&=7&\purple{\text{Step } 3}\\\\
\end{align}$

**Find Nigel's mistake.**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: $\green{\text{Step }1}$ multiplied the right side of the equation by $\dfrac12$ and divided by $\dfrac12$ on the left side of the equation.

This is a mistake!

Both sides of the equation should have been divided by $\dfrac12$ .
Nigel made a mistake in $\green{\text{Step }1}$.

choices.content: Nigel did not make a mistake.

assessment_items.question_content:
Carly tried to solve an equation step by step.

$\qquad\begin{align}
-4(7j+2)&=10\\\\
\\
7j+2&=-40&\green{\text{Step } 1}\\\\
\\
7j&=-42&\blue{\text{Step } 2}\\\\
\\
j&=-6&\purple{\text{Step } 3}\\\\
\end{align}$

**Find Carly's mistake.**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: $\green{\text{Step }1}$ multiplied the right side of the equation by $4$ and divided by $4$ on the left side of the equation.

This is a mistake!

Both sides of the equation should have been divided by $4$ .
Carly made a mistake in $\green{\text{Step }1}$.

choices.content: Carly did not make a mistake.

assessment_items.question_content:
Gabi tried to solve an equation step by step.

$\qquad\begin{align}
0.1(0.5m+6)&=2\\\\
\\
0.5m+6&=20&\green{\text{Step } 1}\\\\
\\
0.5m&=14&\blue{\text{Step } 2}\\\\
\\
m&=7&\purple{\text{Step } 3}\\\\
\end{align}$

**Find Gabi's mistake.**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: $\green{\text{Step }1}$ correctly divided each side of the equation by $0.1$.
$\blue{\text{Step }2}$ correctly subtracted $6$ from each side of the equation.
$\purple{\text{Step }3}$ multiplied the right side of the equation by $0.5$ and divided the left side of the equation by $0.5$.

This is a mistake!

Both sides of the equation should have been divided by $0.5$ .
Gabi made a mistake in $\purple{\text{Step }3}$.

choices.content: Gabi did not make a mistake.

hints: $\green{\text{Step }1}$ correctly divided each side of the equation by $\dfrac14$.
$\blue{\text{Step }2}$ correctly subtracted $9$ from each side of the equation.
$\purple{\text{Step }3}$ multiplied the right side of the equation by $\dfrac13$ and divided the left side of the equation by $\dfrac13$.

This is a mistake!

Both sides of the equation should have been divided by $\dfrac13$ .
Olga made a mistake in $\purple{\text{Step }3}$.

choices.content: Olga did not make a mistake.

assessment_items.question_content:
Jessie tried to solve an equation step by step.

$\begin{align}
1.5b+9&=11\\\\
1.5b&=3&\green{\text{Step } 1}\\\\
b&=2&\blue{\text{Step } 2}
\end{align}$

**Find Jessie's mistake.**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: $\green{\text{Step }1}$ subtracted $9$ from the left side of the equation and subtracted $8$ from the right side.

This is a mistake!

$9$ should have been subtracted from both sides of the equation.
Jessie made a mistake in $\green{\text{Step }1}$.

choices.content: Jessie did not make a mistake.

assessment_items.question_content:
Jamie tried to solve an equation step by step.

$\qquad\begin{align}
\dfrac25n+7&=12\\\\
\\
\dfrac25n&=5&\green{\text{Step } 1}\\\\
\\
n&=2&\blue{\text{Step } 2}\\\\
\end{align}$

**Find Jamie's mistake.**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: $\green{\text{Step }1}$ correctly subtracted $7$ from each side of the equation.
$\blue{\text{Step }2}$ multiplied the right side of the equation by $\dfrac25$ and divided by $\dfrac25$ on the left side of the equation.

This is a mistake!

Both sides of the equation should have been divided by $\dfrac25$.
Jamie made a mistake in $\blue{\text{Step }2}$.

choices.content: Jamie did not make a mistake.

assessment_items.question_content:
Vlad tried to solve an equation step by step.

$\qquad\begin{align}
-8p+14&=42\\\\
\\
-8p&=28&\green{\text{Step } 1}\\\\
\\
p&=-3.5&\blue{\text{Step } 2}\\\\
\end{align}$

**Find Vlad's mistake.**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: $\green{\text{Step }1}$ correctly subtracted $14$ from each side of the equation.
$\blue{\text{Step }2}$ correctly divided each side of the equation by $-8$.
Vlad did not make a mistake.

choices.content: Vlad did not make a mistake.

assessment_items.question_content:
Ling tried to solve an equation step by step.

$\qquad\begin{align}
-2q+11&=-32\\\\
\\
-2q&=-43&\green{\text{Step } 1}\\\\
\\
q&=21.5&\blue{\text{Step } 2}\\\\
\end{align}$

**Find Ling's mistake.**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: $\green{\text{Step }1}$ correctly subtracted $11$ from each side of the equation.
$\blue{\text{Step }2}$ correctly divided each side of the equation by $-2$.
Ling did not make a mistake.

choices.content: Ling did not make a mistake.

assessment_items.question_content:
Yusuf tried to solve an equation step by step.

$\qquad\begin{align}
\dfrac14r+2&=10\\\\
\\
\dfrac14r&=12&\green{\text{Step } 1}\\\\
\\
r&=48&\blue{\text{Step } 2}\\\\
\end{align}$

**Find Yusuf's mistake.**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: $\green{\text{Step }1}$ added $2$ to the right side of the equation and subtracted $2$ from the left side of the equation.

This is a mistake!

$2$ should have been subtracted from both sides of the equation.
Yusuf made a mistake in $\green{\text{Step }1}$.

choices.content: Yusuf did not make a mistake.

assessment_items.question_content:
Mia tried to solve an equation step by step.

$\qquad\begin{align}
0.1t-3&=5\\\\
\\
0.1t&=2&\green{\text{Step } 1}\\\\
\\
t&=20&\blue{\text{Step } 2}\\\\
\end{align}$

**Find Mia's mistake.**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: $\green{\text{Step }1}$ added $3$ to the left side of the equation and subtracted $3$ from the right side of the equation.

This is a mistake!

$3$ should have been added to both sides of the equation.
Mia made a mistake in $\green{\text{Step }1}$.

choices.content: Mia did not make a mistake.

assessment_items.question_content:
Leon tried to solve an equation step by step.

$\qquad\begin{align}
\dfrac12w-12&=18\\\\
\\
\dfrac12w&=30&\green{\text{Step } 1}\\\\
\\
w&=15&\blue{\text{Step } 2}\\\\
\end{align}$

**Find Leon's mistake.**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: $\green{\text{Step }1}$ correctly added $12$ to each side of the equation.
$\blue{\text{Step }2}$ multiplied the right side of the equation by $\dfrac12$ and divided by $\dfrac12$ on the left side of the equation.

This is a mistake!

Both sides of the equation should have been divided by $\dfrac12$.
Leon made a mistake in $\blue{\text{Step }2}$.
